title: Cathedral Products Pack of 20 A4 Gloss Laminating Pouches 150 Micron
features: THICK & STURDY: 150 micron pouches (2 x 75 micron) provide maximum durability and protection for your documents.
CRYSTAL CLEAR GLOSS FINISH: The high-quality gloss finish gives your documents a professional look.
A4 SIZED POUCHES: Perfect for use in any A4 laminator.
MULTIPLE USES: Ideal for business, education, sales material, projects, and important documents.
PROTECTION FROM SPILLS & TEARS: Keep your documents safe from coffee stains, tears, and any other mishaps life throws your way.
description: From the manufacturer Key Features Durable Protection Cathedral Products laminating pouches are made with high-quality materials that provide a durable layer of protection against water, dust, and other environmental factors. Our laminating pouches are available in either A4 or A3 size, making it easy to achieve a professional finish on a huge range of documents. Crystal Clear Finish The glossy finish of our laminating pouches enhances the look of your documents by providing a clear, vibrant finish that is sure to impress. Available as either 150 or 250 micron with a crystal clear gloss finish, these pouches are perfect for preserving everything from photographs to business cards. Easy to Use & Versatile Cathedral Products pouches are compatible with most laminating machines and are designed to be easy to use. Simply insert your document into the pouch and run it through the laminating machine. No carrier needed! Our laminating pouches are suitable for a wide range of applications, including preserving photographs, important documents, and even menus or signage. Frequently Asked Questions Q: Can Cathedral Products laminating pouches be used with any laminator? A: Yes! Our laminating pouches are compatible with all hot laminators. Q: Do I need to use a carrier with Cathedral Products laminating pouches? A: Nope! Our laminating pouches are for use without a carrier! Q: My laminated documents are curling! Are my pouches faulty? A: Not at all! It could be that the laminator is not placed on a flat surface, or it may be that the temperature of your laminator is too hot.
